skill.name: ai-sdk-handler
skill.description: Guides implementation of Vercel AI SDK features for chat, structured output, generative UI, and background agents.
skill.summary: Build Next.js AI features with Vercel AI SDK patterns.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many issues, but the shell execution and weak cryptography matches are false positives from markdown code fences, inline code, and TypeScript template strings. Confirmed risks are normal for an AI SDK integration skill: environment-based provider keys, outbound model calls, and sample AI routes that need authentication and rate limits. No evidence found of prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sample AI API Routes Need Access Controls
medium_findings.description: Several examples define POST handlers that call AI models without showing authentication, authorization, or rate limiting. This can expose paid model endpoints to abuse if copied directly into production.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples contain complete route handlers that parse requests and call AI SDK functions without an auth wrapper. The risk depends on whether users add controls before deployment.

medium_findings.title: User Input Is Sent To LLM Providers
medium_findings.description: The examples pass request messages and prompts into model calls. This is expected for the skill, but production usage needs validation, prompt-injection handling, logging review, and cost controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code examples clearly forward request-derived values into AI SDK calls. This is a legitimate AI pattern, but it creates predictable data exposure and abuse risks.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown inline code, markdown code fences, or TypeScript template strings. No evidence found that the skill executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation and example TypeScript, not executable skill code. The skill contains no script file or command runner.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited locations do not contain cryptographic functions or weak hash algorithms. They are prose or AI SDK examples, so the blocker classification is not supported by the file context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography usage. The static pattern appears to have matched unrelated text.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Static Network Finding Is Low Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are documentation links to AI SDK documentation. They do not send local data or credentials to a remote service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain markdown comments or links to public documentation. There is no runtime fetch or hidden outbound request at those lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Provider API Keys Loaded From Environment
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference configuration reads OpenAI and Anthropic API keys from environment variables. This is the correct server-side pattern, but these variables must never be exposed to client components or logs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key access is explicit and limited to provider configuration. It is legitimate, but mishandling would expose credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Model Calls Through AI SDK
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples call AI SDK generation and streaming functions that contact model providers. This network behavior is core to the skill and should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples use AI SDK provider models in server handlers and background steps. Provider calls are expected but can transmit user content externally.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable patterns for adding AI features to Next.js apps. This skill provides focused guidance for chat, structured output, generative UI, and background agents using Vercel AI SDK.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Vercel AI SDK for chat, text, JSON, and agent workflows.
Provides Next.js route handler patterns for streaming chat responses.
Shows structured object generation with Zod schemas.
Demonstrates generative UI patterns with server-rendered components.
Covers background agent workflows with Inngest for longer tasks.
Documents provider setup for OpenAI and Anthropic integrations.
limitations: Examples are documentation snippets, not a complete production application.
Some examples omit authentication and rate limiting for brevity.
It does not implement image or video generation workflows.
It assumes a Next.js App Router project and related local conventions.

use_cases.title: Add Streaming Chat To A Product
use_cases.description: Use the chat route and client patterns to add responsive AI conversations to a Next.js application.

use_cases.title: Extract Structured Data From User Input
use_cases.description: Use Zod-backed object generation to turn prompts or documents into validated application data.

use_cases.title: Run Longer AI Tasks In The Background
use_cases.description: Use the Inngest examples to plan, run, and summarize multi-step agent work outside request timeouts.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Chat Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a first AI endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Vercel AI SDK streaming chat route to my Next.js App Router project. Include provider setup and a simple client component.

prompt_templates.title: Add Structured Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use generateObject with a Zod schema to convert user input into typed data. Show validation boundaries and server-side handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design Generative UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced server-rendered UI from model tool calls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a generative UI route with streamUI. Include a tool schema, a component response, and clear error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Background Agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with long-running reasoning steps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Inngest-based AI agent using Vercel AI SDK. Include loop limits, step names, persistence points, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: Request to add streaming chat to an authenticated dashboard.
output_examples.output: A route plan, provider configuration notes, client component structure, and reminders for auth, logging, and rate limits.

output_examples.input: Request to extract itinerary details from a free-form destination prompt.
output_examples.output: A schema-first object generation approach with typed fields, validation expectations, and response handling guidance.

output_examples.input: Request to move a research assistant beyond serverless request limits.
output_examples.output: A background workflow outline with planning, bounded iteration, synthesis, storage, and user notification steps.

best_practices: Keep provider keys on the server and read them only from environment variables.
Protect AI routes with authentication, authorization, rate limits, and usage logging.
Use schemas, bounded loops, and clear prompts for predictable model behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not expose AI SDK provider keys in client components or browser bundles.
Do not deploy open chat routes without abuse prevention and cost controls.
Do not allow unbounded agent loops or uncontrolled tool execution.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js App Router projects that use Vercel AI SDK for server-side AI features.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata marks it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it include production-ready authentication?
faq.answer: It recommends protected routes, but some examples are simplified and need production access controls.

faq.question: Can it generate images or video?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that image and video generation should use a separate ai-handler skill.

faq.question: Which providers are shown?
faq.answer: The reference shows OpenAI and Anthropic provider configuration through the AI SDK.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it documents external model calls, API keys, and sample routes that need safeguards.
